subject: Sociology
title: Functionalist Theory
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Functionalist Theory.

hook.text: A surgeon earns more than a janitor. Functionalists say that's necessary — society rewards the jobs it needs most.

buildup.text: Davis and Moore argued in 1945 that inequality motivates people to fill important, difficult positions.

discovery.text: Their logic: without higher rewards, no one would endure years of training to become a doctor or engineer.

twist.text: Critics ask: who decides which jobs are important? Teachers and nurses are essential but poorly paid.

climax.text: Functionalism explains some inequality but ignores power: the wealthy shape which positions get rewarded and how.

punchline.text: Rewards don't follow importance. They follow power.

quiz.question: What does functionalist theory claim about inequality?
options: It motivates people to fill important positions
It is always harmful and unnecessary
It is caused entirely by discrimination
